question: [File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/resources/graphql/query.graphqls -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: account(input: AccountInput!)] Can an unprivileged external user observe that `pendingReward` is stale (updated only at end of staking period) and use the `stakePeriodStart` timestamp to calculate the exact moment when `entity_stake.pending_reward` will be updated, timing a reward-stealing transaction to execute immediately after the update?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/resources/graphql/query.graphqls -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: account(input: AccountInput!)] Can an unprivileged external user query `pendingReward` for an account where `declineReward: false` and `stakePeriodStart` is many periods in the past, and use this to identify accounts that have never claimed rewards and have large accumulated `pendingReward` values, targeting them for theft?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/resources/graphql/query.graphqls -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: account(input: AccountInput!)] Can an unprivileged external user use GraphQL introspection to discover the `pendingReward` field on the `Account` type and its `unit: HbarUnit` argument, then systematically query all accounts to build a reward map without any authentication barrier?
